svg.text: • Constraint solvers (Z3, MiniSAT, OR-Tools): DPLL is backtracking + unit propagation.
• Compiler register allocation: graph coloring via backtracking.
• Crossword / puzzle generation: assign words under length + cross constraints.
• ML hyperparameter search (legacy): grid + prune dominated configs.
• Layout engines: tableau / floorplan packers backtrack on infeasible placements.
• Type inference: Hindley–Milner unification rolls back on conflict.
• Theorem provers (Coq, Lean): tactic search backtracks on unprovable subgoals.
✓ Search space is combinatorial (subsets, permutations, assignments).
✓ Constraints reject many partial states early — pruning is effective.
✓ Need ALL solutions, or to enumerate, or proof of no solution.
✗ Polynomial DP exists — use that instead (knapsack with small values).
✗ Greedy is provably optimal — skip the search.
✗ Need approximate answer fast — use local search (simulated annealing, beam).
Rule: backtracking is a structured way to enumerate; pruning is what makes it fast.
